This page is generated from the current module specs, examples, and Lua API data.

**Module group:** Feature Systems
**Namespace:** `lurek.cursor`

## 🎯 Purpose

Cursor management: system cursors, custom images, animated cursors, trails, context switching, zoom.

[⬆ back to top](#table-of-contents)

## 📋 Summary

Cursor management: system cursors, custom images, animated cursors, trails, context switching, zoom.

## 🧩 Key Types

- `LAnimatedCursor` (8 methods) - Lua userdata representing an animated cursor that cycles through image frames.
- `LCursorManager` (18 methods) - Lua userdata that controls cursor appearance and system cursor selection.
- `LCustomCursor` (4 methods) - Lua userdata representing a custom-drawn cursor image with a configurable hot-spot.

[⬆ back to top](#table-of-contents)

## ⚙️ Module Functions

### Module-Level Functions

#### lurek.cursor.newAnimated

#### Definition

```lua
--- Creates a new animated cursor that can cycle through frames.
---@param looping boolean Whether the animation loops continuously.
---@return LAnimatedCursor A new animated cursor instance.
lurek.cursor.newAnimated = function(looping) end
```

#### Description

Creates a new animated cursor that can cycle through frames.

Parameters:

- `looping` (`boolean`, required): Whether the animation loops continuously.

Returns: `LAnimatedCursor` - A new animated cursor instance.

#### Example

Source: [cursor.lua](../blob/main/content/examples/cursor.lua)

```lua
do
    local c = lurek.cursor.newAnimated(true)
    print("animated frame count = " .. c:frameCount())
    print("animated scale = " .. c:currentScale())
end
```

#### lurek.cursor.newCustom

#### Definition

```lua
--- Creates a new custom cursor with specified dimensions and hotspot position.
---@param w number Width of the cursor image in pixels.
---@param h number Height of the cursor image in pixels.
---@param hx number Hotspot X offset from cursor origin.
---@param hy number Hotspot Y offset from cursor origin.
---@return LCustomCursor A new custom cursor instance.
lurek.cursor.newCustom = function(w, h, hx, hy) end
```

#### Description

Creates a new custom cursor with specified dimensions and hotspot position.

Parameters:

- `w` (`integer`, required): Width of the cursor image in pixels.
- `h` (`integer`, required): Height of the cursor image in pixels.
- `hx` (`integer`, required): Hotspot X offset from cursor origin.
- `hy` (`integer`, required): Hotspot Y offset from cursor origin.

Returns: `LCustomCursor` - A new custom cursor instance.

#### Example

Source: [cursor.lua](../blob/main/content/examples/cursor.lua)

```lua
do
    local c = lurek.cursor.newCustom(16, 16, 0, 0)
    local w, h = c:getSize()
    print("custom cursor size = " .. w .. "x" .. h)
    print("hotspot = 0,0")
end
```

#### lurek.cursor.newManager

#### Definition

```lua
--- Creates a new cursor manager for handling cursor state and visibility.
---@return LCursorManager A new cursor manager instance.
lurek.cursor.newManager = function() end
```

#### Description

Creates a new cursor manager for handling cursor state and visibility.

Returns: `LCursorManager` - A new cursor manager instance.

#### Example

Source: [cursor.lua](../blob/main/content/examples/cursor.lua)

```lua
do
    local cm = lurek.cursor.newManager()
    print("manager visible = " .. tostring(cm:isVisible()))
    print("manager context = " .. cm:getContext())
end
```

#### lurek.cursor.systemCursors

#### Definition

```lua
--- Returns a list of all available system cursor names as a string array.
---@return table Array of system cursor name strings.
lurek.cursor.systemCursors = function() end
```

#### Description

Returns a list of all available system cursor names as a string array.

Returns: `table` - Array of system cursor name strings.

#### Example

Source: [cursor.lua](../blob/main/content/examples/cursor.lua)

```lua
do
    local list = lurek.cursor.systemCursors()
    print("lurek.cursor.systemCursors count=" .. #list)
end
```

## 🔹 Module Methods

### LAnimatedCursor Methods

#### LAnimatedCursor:addFrame

#### Definition

```lua
--- Add a frame from a custom cursor image.
---@param cursor LuaCustomCursor Frame image.
---@param duration_ms number Frame duration in milliseconds.
function LAnimatedCursor:addFrame(cursor, duration_ms) end
```

#### Description

Add a frame from a custom cursor image.

Parameters:

- `cursor` (`LuaCustomCursor`, required): Frame image.
- `duration_ms` (`integer`, required): Frame duration in milliseconds.

#### Example

Source: [cursor.lua](../blob/main/content/examples/cursor.lua)

```lua
do
    local c = lurek.cursor.newAnimated(true)
    local frame = lurek.cursor.newCustom(16, 16, 0, 0)
    c:addFrame(frame, 100)
    print("LAnimatedCursor:addFrame count=" .. c:frameCount())
end
```

#### LAnimatedCursor:clearPulse

#### Definition

```lua
--- Disable pulse animation for this object.
function LAnimatedCursor:clearPulse() end
```

#### Description

Disable pulse animation for this object.

#### Example

Source: [cursor.lua](../blob/main/content/examples/cursor.lua)

```lua
do
    local c = lurek.cursor.newAnimated(true)
    c:setPulse(0.8, 1.2, 1.5)
    c:clearPulse()
    print("LAnimatedCursor:clearPulse scale=" .. c:currentScale())
end
```

#### LAnimatedCursor:frameCount

#### Definition

```lua
--- Get total frame count for this object.
---@return number Number of frames.
function LAnimatedCursor:frameCount() end
```

#### Description

Get total frame count for this object.

Returns: `integer` - Number of frames.

#### Example

Source: [cursor.lua](../blob/main/content/examples/cursor.lua)

```lua
do
    local c = lurek.cursor.newAnimated(true)
    c:addFrame(lurek.cursor.newCustom(16, 16, 0, 0), 100)
    c:addFrame(lurek.cursor.newCustom(16, 16, 0, 0), 100)
    print("LAnimatedCursor:frameCount=" .. c:frameCount())
end
```

#### LAnimatedCursor:reset

#### Definition

```lua
--- Reset the cursor animation playback to the first frame.
function LAnimatedCursor:reset() end
```

#### Description

Reset the cursor animation playback to the first frame.

#### Example

Source: [cursor.lua](../blob/main/content/examples/cursor.lua)

```lua
do
    local c = lurek.cursor.newAnimated(true)
    c:addFrame(lurek.cursor.newCustom(16, 16, 0, 0), 100)
    c:update(0.2)
    c:reset()
    print("LAnimatedCursor:reset idx=" .. c:currentIndex())
end
```

#### LAnimatedCursor:setPulse

#### Definition

```lua
--- Set the pulse animation speed and scale factor parameters.
---@param min_scale number Minimum scale.
---@param max_scale number Maximum scale.
---@param speed number Pulse speed.
function LAnimatedCursor:setPulse(min_scale, max_scale, speed) end
```

#### Description

Set the pulse animation speed and scale factor parameters.

Parameters:

- `min_scale` (`number`, required): Minimum scale.
- `max_scale` (`number`, required): Maximum scale.
- `speed` (`number`, required): Pulse speed.

#### Example

Source: [cursor.lua](../blob/main/content/examples/cursor.lua)

```lua
do
    local c = lurek.cursor.newAnimated(true)
    c:setPulse(0.8, 1.2, 1.5)
    print("LAnimatedCursor:setPulse scale=" .. c:currentScale())
end
```

#### LAnimatedCursor:update

#### Definition

```lua
--- Update animation (call each frame).
---@param dt number Delta time in seconds.
function LAnimatedCursor:update(dt) end
```

#### Description

Update animation (call each frame).

Parameters:

- `dt` (`number`, required): Delta time in seconds.

#### Example

Source: [cursor.lua](../blob/main/content/examples/cursor.lua)

```lua
do
    local c = lurek.cursor.newAnimated(true)
    local frame = lurek.cursor.newCustom(16, 16, 0, 0)
    c:addFrame(frame, 100)
    c:update(0.05)
    print("LAnimatedCursor:update idx=" .. c:currentIndex())
end
```

### LCursorManager Methods

#### LCursorManager:addRule

#### Definition

```lua
--- Add a context rule that maps a context to a system cursor.
---@param ctx string Context name.
---@param cursor_name string System cursor name.
function LCursorManager:addRule(ctx, cursor_name) end
```

#### Description

Add a context rule that maps a context to a system cursor.

Parameters:

- `ctx` (`string`, required): Context name.
- `cursor_name` (`string`, required): System cursor name.

#### Example

Source: [cursor.lua](../blob/main/content/examples/cursor.lua)

```lua
do
    local cm = lurek.cursor.newManager()
    cm:addRule("gameplay", "crosshair")
    cm:setContext("gameplay")
    print("LCursorManager:addRule ok")
    print("context = " .. cm:getContext())
end
```

#### LCursorManager:disableTrail

#### Definition

```lua
--- Disable cursor trail for this object.
function LCursorManager:disableTrail() end
```

#### Description

Disable cursor trail for this object.

#### Example

Source: [cursor.lua](../blob/main/content/examples/cursor.lua)

```lua
do
    local cm = lurek.cursor.newManager()
    cm:enableTrail(1.0, 1.0, 1.0, 0.5)
    cm:disableTrail()
    print("LCursorManager:disableTrail ok")
end
```

#### LCursorManager:disableZoom

#### Definition

```lua
--- Disable cursor zoom for this object.
function LCursorManager:disableZoom() end
```

Disable cursor zoom for this object.

#### Example

Source: [cursor.lua](../blob/main/content/examples/cursor.lua)

```lua
do
    local cm = lurek.cursor.newManager()
    cm:enableZoom(1.5, 60)
    cm:disableZoom()
    print("LCursorManager:disableZoom ok")
end
```

#### LCursorManager:enableLineTrail

#### Definition

```lua
--- Enable cursor trail with line mode.
---@param r number Red (0-1).
---@param g number Green (0-1).
---@param b number Blue (0-1).
---@param width number Line width in pixels.
function LCursorManager:enableLineTrail(r, g, b, width) end
```

#### Description

Enable cursor trail with line mode.

Parameters:

- `r` (`number`, required): Red (0-1).
- `g` (`number`, required): Green (0-1).
- `b` (`number`, required): Blue (0-1).
- `width` (`number`, required): Line width in pixels.

#### Example

Source: [cursor.lua](../blob/main/content/examples/cursor.lua)

```lua
do
    local cm = lurek.cursor.newManager()
    cm:enableLineTrail(0.0, 1.0, 1.0, 2.0)
    print("LCursorManager:enableLineTrail ok")
    print("locked = " .. tostring(cm:isLocked()))
end
```

#### LCursorManager:enableTrail

#### Definition

```lua
--- Enable cursor trail with fade points mode.
---@param r number Red (0-1).
---@param g number Green (0-1).
---@param b number Blue (0-1).
---@param lifetime number Seconds before trail fades.
function LCursorManager:enableTrail(r, g, b, lifetime) end
```

#### Description

Enable cursor trail with fade points mode.

Parameters:

- `r` (`number`, required): Red (0-1).
- `g` (`number`, required): Green (0-1).
- `b` (`number`, required): Blue (0-1).
- `lifetime` (`number`, required): Seconds before trail fades.

#### Example

Source: [cursor.lua](../blob/main/content/examples/cursor.lua)

```lua
do
    local cm = lurek.cursor.newManager()
    cm:enableTrail(1.0, 0.5, 0.0, 0.8)
    print("LCursorManager:enableTrail ok")
    print("visible = " .. tostring(cm:isVisible()))
end
```

#### LCursorManager:enableZoom

#### Definition

```lua
--- Enable zoom/magnifier at cursor position.
---@param magnification number Zoom factor (1-10).
---@param radius number Lens radius in pixels.
function LCursorManager:enableZoom(magnification, radius) end
```

#### Description

Enable zoom/magnifier at cursor position.

Parameters:

- `magnification` (`number`, required): Zoom factor (1-10).
- `radius` (`number`, required): Lens radius in pixels.

#### Example

Source: [cursor.lua](../blob/main/content/examples/cursor.lua)

```lua
do
    local cm = lurek.cursor.newManager()
    cm:enableZoom(2.0, 80)
    print("LCursorManager:enableZoom ok")
    print("context = " .. cm:getContext())
end
```

#### LCursorManager:update

#### Definition

```lua
--- Update cursor state (call each frame).
---@param x number Cursor X position.
---@param y number Cursor Y position.
---@param dt number Delta time in seconds.
function LCursorManager:update(x, y, dt) end
```

#### Description

Update cursor state (call each frame).

Parameters:

- `x` (`number`, required): Cursor X position.
- `y` (`number`, required): Cursor Y position.
- `dt` (`number`, required): Delta time in seconds.

#### Example

Source: [cursor.lua](../blob/main/content/examples/cursor.lua)

```lua
do
    local cm = lurek.cursor.newManager()
    cm:update(320, 180, 0.016)
    local x, y = cm:getPosition()
    print("LCursorManager:update ok")
    print("position = " .. x .. ", " .. y)
end
```

### LCustomCursor Methods

#### LCustomCursor:getHotspot

#### Definition

```lua
--- Get hotspot position for this object.
---@return number a Hotspot X.
---@return number b Hotspot Y.
function LCustomCursor:getHotspot() end
```

#### Description

Get hotspot position for this object.

Returns: `integer` - Hotspot X.

#### Example

Source: [cursor.lua](../blob/main/content/examples/cursor.lua)

```lua
do
    local c = lurek.cursor.newCustom(32, 32, 16, 16)
    local hx, hy = c:getHotspot()
    print("LCustomCursor:getHotspot hx=" .. hx .. " hy=" .. hy)
end
```

#### LCustomCursor:getPixel

#### Definition

```lua
--- Get the pixel color at the specified cursor image position.
---@param x number X coordinate.
---@param y number Y coordinate.
---@return number a Red.
---@return number b Green.
---@return number c Blue.
---@return number d Alpha.
function LCustomCursor:getPixel(x, y) end
```

#### Description

Get the pixel color at the specified cursor image position.

Parameters:

- `x` (`integer`, required): X coordinate.
- `y` (`integer`, required): Y coordinate.

Returns: `integer` - Red.

#### Example

Source: [cursor.lua](../blob/main/content/examples/cursor.lua)

```lua
do
    local c = lurek.cursor.newCustom(16, 16, 0, 0)
    c:setPixel(4, 4, 255, 0, 0, 255)
    local r, g, b, a = c:getPixel(4, 4)
    print("LCustomCursor:getPixel r=" .. r)
    print("pixel rgba = " .. r .. "," .. g .. "," .. b .. "," .. a)
end
```

#### Definition

```lua

---@return number a Width.

function LCustomCursor:getSize() end
```

```lua
do
    local c = lurek.cursor.newCustom(24, 24, 12, 12)
    local w, h = c:getSize()
    print("LCustomCursor:getSize=" .. w .. "x" .. h)
end
```

#### LCustomCursor:setPixel

#### Definition

```lua
--- Set a pixel color — Lua userdata object exposed by the engine.
---@param x number X coordinate.
---@param y number Y coordinate.
---@param r number Red (0-255).
---@param g number Green (0-255).
---@param b number Blue (0-255).
---@param a number Alpha (0-255).
function LCustomCursor:setPixel(x, y, r, g, b, a) end
```

#### Description

Set a pixel color — Lua userdata object exposed by the engine.

Parameters:

- `x` (`integer`, required): X coordinate.
- `y` (`integer`, required): Y coordinate.
- `r` (`integer`, required): Red (0-255).
- `g` (`integer`, required): Green (0-255).
- `b` (`integer`, required): Blue (0-255).
- `a` (`integer`, required): Alpha (0-255).

#### Example

Source: [cursor.lua](../blob/main/content/examples/cursor.lua)

```lua
do
    local c = lurek.cursor.newCustom(16, 16, 0, 0)
    c:setPixel(8, 8, 255, 255, 255, 255)
    print("LCustomCursor:setPixel ok")
end
```

## 🔗 Related Modules

- Previous: [compute](Module-compute)
- Next: [dataframe](Module-dataframe)
- [ai](Module-ai) - Game AI toolkit: FSMs, behaviour trees, GOAP, steering, utility AI, blackboards. Pure CPU.
- [animation](Module-animation) - Sprite animation: source-rect changes over time. Imports only math; headless-testable.
- [automation](Module-automation) - Automated input simulation for headless tests, QA replay, recorded sessions.
- [charts](Module-charts) - Software-rasterized chart renderers (line, bar, scatter, pie, area) to RGBA8 pixel buffers.
- [dialog](Module-dialog) - Dialog/conversation engine: weighted topic/branch selection, state tracking, speaker registry.
- [ecs](Module-ecs) - Entity-Component-System: identity / data / behaviour separation for runtime composition.
